rulings.Case Title: LETICIA MEDINA VS. LINH THIEU LIEU TRAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Reopen Discovery
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, August 31, 2015, Line 2, DEFENDANT LINH LIEU TRAN'S Motion To Reopen Discovery. Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion granted in part; denied in part. Defendant may serve additional third party subpoenas concerning the subsequent accidents and Plaintiffs' medical treatment. However, any other discovery beyond those subpoenas, including further depositions, is denied at this juncture. After receipt of the third party subpoenas, Defendant may make a subsequent motion for more discovery, but must articulate additional good cause and the other factors required by Regan v. Lanet (1987) 197 Cal.App.3d 353. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davidmcdonald@prodigy.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ENCARNITA ALONSO VS. GURBAKSH CHAHAL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Arbitration And For Stay
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, August 31, 2015, Line 10, DEFENDANTS GURBAKSH CHAHAL AND GRAVITY4, INC.'S Motion For Order Compelling Arbitration And For Stay. Defendants' motion to compel arbitration and for stay is GRANTED. Plaintiff fails to show that the arbitration agreement is procedurally and substantively unconscionable. Plaintiff is a highly compensated senior business executive. The arbitration rules were accessible to her within seconds. The arbitration agreement is clearly labeled "Agreement to Arbitrate". The cases that fault arbitration agreements for making some claims arbitrable and others not regard "weak" employees, not executives like Plaintiff. Moreover, the carveout here is of intellectual property claims as to which most arbitrators lack expertise and/or the expeditious ability to grant injunctive relief. The arbitration terms are not unreasonable; indeed, Defendants are obligated to pay all arbitration costs, even those of Plaintiff. Plaintiff argues that arbitration in San Francisco is unfair, but this is where she filed suit. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RU)

rulings.Case Title: JOONG Y. IM VS. ILONA BRUSIL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Application And Hearing For Right To Attach Order And Writ Of Attachment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, August 31, 2015, Line 12, PLAINTIFF JOONG IM'S Application And Hearing For Right To Attach Order And Writ Of Attachment. Plaintiff's application for writ of attachment and right to attach order is DENIED. Plaintiff has not shown "it is more likely than not that the plaintiff will obtain judgment against the defendant on the claim." See CCP sec. 481.190.
